name: telegram
description: Telegram bot (grammY): message it and it answers from Elowen AI. Slash commands (/model, /reasoning, /display, /new, /help), per-chat model and presentation settings, live tool activity with independent answer delivery, status reactions and proactive cron/tick pushes. Chat tools (admin sessions): chat & member info, member count, pin/unpin, delete messages, ban/unban, promote, set title/description, forum topics (create/edit/close), plus send a message to any chat (operator). Each rolePolicy maps a Telegram user id / @username / chat id to allowed Elowen projects + a role prompt.

configSchema.label: Connection
configSchema.hint: The bot token and where it listens.

configSchema.label: Bot token
configSchema.hint: Telegram → @BotFather → /newbot (or an existing bot's token). To let the bot see all group messages, disable BotFather → Bot Settings → Group Privacy.

configSchema.label: Allowed chats
configSchema.hint: Comma-separated chat IDs (a numeric user/group id, e.g. 123456789 or -1001234567890). Empty = respond everywhere allowed. When set, the bot only responds in these chats.

configSchema.label: Notification chat ID
configSchema.hint: Where proactive messages (cron/tick results, escalations) are posted — a numeric chat id or @channelusername. Empty = no proactive pushes.

configSchema.label: Replies & behavior
configSchema.hint: When and how the bot answers.

configSchema.label: Respond without mention
configSchema.hint: On: in groups the bot answers every message from a mapped sender. Off: in groups it only answers when @mentioned or when its message is replied to. Direct chats always get an answer.

configSchema.label: Tool activity
configSchema.hint: Off hides the work trace. Live status updates tool start/completion. Live output also streams bounded run_command progress. Override per chat with /display.

configSchema.label: Answer delivery
configSchema.hint: Final posts one complete answer below the live tool trace. Live edits the answer while it is written. Override per chat with /display.

configSchema.label: Tool output detail
configSchema.hint: Controls how much bounded tool output appears below each status. Raw full output is never dumped into Telegram.

configSchema.label: Tool message layout
configSchema.hint: One live message keeps the chat compact. One message per tool gives every tool call its own editable Telegram message.

configSchema.label: Status reactions
configSchema.hint: React 👀 while thinking, 👍/👎 when done (Telegram allows only a limited reaction emoji set; unsupported ones are skipped).

configSchema.label: Runtime footer
configSchema.hint: Append a small "model · context %" line under the final reply.

configSchema.label: Show reasoning
configSchema.hint: Stream the model's reasoning into the progress message (extended-thinking models). Off by default — it's usually noise.

configSchema.label: Service language
configSchema.hint: Language of the bot's own service messages (/new, /model, placeholders): en (default) or cs.

configSchema.label: Conversation
configSchema.hint: Image routing.

configSchema.label: Vision model
configSchema.hint: Model used for turns that carry image attachments (must support vision). Empty = the chat's normal model.

configSchema.label: Media limits
configSchema.hint: Caps on inbound/outbound images and how long a pending question stays open.

configSchema.label: Max inbound image size
configSchema.hint: Largest image attachment (bytes) the bot downloads for vision; bigger ones are noted instead.

configSchema.label: Max images per message
configSchema.hint: How many image attachments in a single incoming message the bot sends to the vision model.

configSchema.label: Max images per reply
configSchema.hint: How many generated images the bot attaches to a single outgoing reply.

configSchema.label: Question timeout (ms)
configSchema.hint: How long a pending ask_user_question stays open for a button tap or free-text reply before it's dropped as stale.

configSchema.label: Voice
configSchema.hint: Speech-to-text and text-to-speech (optional; reuses the voice provider's key).

configSchema.label: Voice provider
configSchema.hint: Which configured OpenAI-compatible provider powers voice (Whisper STT + TTS) — reuses that provider's key, no separate key here. Add providers in Settings → Brain. Leave empty to keep voice off.

configSchema.label: Transcribe voice messages
configSchema.hint: Transcribe incoming voice messages / audio attachments with Whisper so the agent understands them. Needs the OpenAI API key.

configSchema.label: Speech-to-text model
configSchema.hint: Whisper transcription model (default: whisper-1).

configSchema.label: Speak replies (default)
configSchema.hint: Default for chats: attach a spoken-audio version of replies as a voice note. Toggle live per chat with /voice. Needs the OpenAI API key.

configSchema.label: Text-to-speech model
configSchema.hint: TTS model (default: gpt-4o-mini-tts).

configSchema.label: TTS voice
configSchema.hint: Voice: alloy, echo, fable, onyx, nova or shimmer (default: alloy).

configSchema.label: Role policies
configSchema.hint: Map Telegram senders to allowed projects, a role prompt and the tools the bot may use.

configSchema.label: Role policies
configSchema.hint: First matching policy wins; senders with no mapped policy are ignored. The ID is a Telegram user ID (123456789), an @username, or a whole chat ID (…negative for groups) — a chat ID grants access to everyone in it. Mark a policy admin to let it switch the model and use the chat tools. Each policy may also restrict which tools the bot can use for it.
